hypothesis: Incorporating an uncertainty-aware confidence estimate from Thermo-Calc into the DPO reward signal—specifically by down-weighting preference pairs drawn from compositional regions where the TCHEA7 database has sparse empirical coverage—will reduce reward hacking and out-of-distribution drift in architecturally sensitive models like OLMo, resulting in more consistent preference tuning improvements across all three local language models without sacrificing design-space coverage.
